Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-06/Vespers/3.firstExpected: Ant. Jacob autem * génuit Joseph virum Maríæ; de qua natus est Jesus, qui vocátur Christus.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-06/Vespers/3.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Help/Rubrics/General Rubrics.html:74-82, 465-469 and upstream/web/www/horas/Help/Rubrics/Tables 1960.txt:49, 75-79, 118-121 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:1-18 — under the 1960 concurrence rules, Epiphany is a feast of the 1st class while Holy Family is a feast of the 2nd class; in concurrence the Vespers of the higher class prevail. Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-09/Matins/29.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:1-2 — the corpus rubric carries guillemets around both `Pater Noster` and `Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem:`. Perl strips them while the compositor preserves them; both are surface renderings of the same rubric sentence, not a liturgical-content disagreement.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-09/Matins/29.notes: Representative entry for the Roman Matins pre-lesson guillemet family under Rubrics 1960. Fan out to all 1960 Matins rows with the same first-divergence text.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-09/None/318c.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:4-8 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Doxologies.txt:1-20 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:664-672 and docs/phase-2-rubrical-engine-design.md:1469 — the Roman January offices set `Doxology=Epi` or a local `[Doxology]`, and fallback hymn resolution is supposed to substitute the seasonal stanza from `Psalterium/Doxologies` in place of the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close. The compositor follows that source-backed rule; the Perl comparison surface retains the unsubstituted fallback stanza.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-19/Lauds/2af.firstExpected: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: * introíte portas ejus in confessióne, átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-19/Lauds/2af.firstActual: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-19/Lauds/2af.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmorum/Psalm99.txt:3-5 — the psalm corpus explicitly encodes 99:3b as `Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ (4a) introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.` The compositor preserves that half-verse structure while removing the numeric carry marker; the Perl comparison surface flattens the same line to a single `*` split.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-19/Lauds/2af.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Lauds Psalm 99 half-verse family where the corpus keeps a `‡ ... *` structure and Perl flattens it to a single `*` boundary.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-05-19/Vespers/3.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Epiphany-octave Vespers fifth-psalm override family where the inherited Rule section replaces the Day0 fifth slot with Psalm 116.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-24/Matins/29.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:1-2 — the corpus rubric carries guillemets around both `Pater Noster` and `Et ne nos indúcas in tentatiónem:`. Perl strips them while the compositor preserves them; both are surface renderings of the same rubric sentence, not a liturgical-content disagreement.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-24/Matins/29.notes: Representative entry for the Roman Matins pre-lesson guillemet family under Rubrics 1960. Fan out to all 1960 Matins rows with the same first-divergence text.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Compline/.firstExpected: Examen conscientiæ vel Pater Noster totum secreto.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Compline/.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:129 — the corpus source carries `«Pater Noster»` with guillemets. Both Perl (strip) and compositor (preserve) are defensible renderings of the same source token; neither is liturgically wrong. Phase 3 §3h defers to the corpus author's formatting.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Compline/.notes: Pattern affects every Compline row across every policy and date. This single entry is representative; the full row-key fan-out is a follow-up bulk-populate task (see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md).

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Lauds/2af.firstExpected: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: * introíte portas ejus in confessióne, átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Lauds/2af.firstActual: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Lauds/2af.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmorum/Psalm99.txt:3-5 — the psalm corpus explicitly encodes 99:3b as `Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ (4a) introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.` The compositor preserves that half-verse structure while removing the numeric carry marker; the Perl comparison surface flattens the same line to a single `*` split.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Lauds/2af.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Lauds Psalm 99 half-verse family where the corpus keeps a `‡ ... *` structure and Perl flattens it to a single `*` boundary.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Vespers/2.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi major.txt:15-20 — the Roman Sunday `Day0 Vespera` table explicitly runs `109,110,111,112,113`, so the second emitted heading is source-backed `Psalmus 110 [2]`. The Perl comparison surface skips ahead to `Psalmus 112 [2]`.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-29/Vespers/2.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Roman January Vespers second-psalm heading family where the source-backed Day0 sequence keeps Psalm 110 in the second slot. Fan out to matching 1960 rows with the same first-divergence pair.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-30/None/4a1a.firstActual: R.br. Clamávi in toto corde meo: * Exáudi me, Dómine.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-30/None/4a1a.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:66-80 — the Sunday None later block consists of the chapter, the short responsory beginning `R.br. Clamávi in toto corde meo...`, and the versicle, with no underscore separator lines in the source. The compositor now emits that source-backed sequence; the Perl comparison surface inserts `_` separator lines into the rendered stream.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-30/None/4a1a.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Jan 14 None later-block separator family where Perl inserts underscore-only lines around the short responsory even though the source section has none.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-30/Prime/553.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi minor.txt:218 — the `Tridentinum` Sunday Prime row explicitly lists `53,117,118(1-16),118(17-32)`. Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-06-30/Prime/553.notes: Representative entry for the Roman Jan 14 Sunday Prime psalm-table family where the source-backed `Tridentinum` row begins with Psalm 53 before Psalm 117.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-07-06/None/318c.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:4-8 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Doxologies.txt:1-20 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:664-672 and docs/phase-2-rubrical-engine-design.md:1469 — the Roman January offices set `Doxology=Epi` or a local `[Doxology]`, and fallback hymn resolution is supposed to substitute the seasonal stanza from `Psalterium/Doxologies` in place of the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close. The compositor follows that source-backed rule; the Perl comparison surface retains the unsubstituted fallback stanza.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-07-06/None/318c.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 January Terce/Sext/None fallback-hymn doxology family where the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close is replaced by the Christmas or Epiphany stanza required by the winning office.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-12-25/None/318c.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:4-8 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Doxologies.txt:1-20 and upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:664-672 and docs/phase-2-rubrical-engine-design.md:1469 — the Roman January offices set `Doxology=Epi` or a local `[Doxology]`, and fallback hymn resolution is supposed to substitute the seasonal stanza from `Psalterium/Doxologies` in place of the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close. The compositor follows that source-backed rule; the Perl comparison surface retains the unsubstituted fallback stanza.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-12-25/None/318c.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 January Terce/Sext/None fallback-hymn doxology family where the default `Præsta, Pater piíssime,` close is replaced by the Christmas or Epiphany stanza required by the winning office.
